title: phalangium glaucum
intro: Phalangium glaucum, also known as the blue caterpillar or caterpillar fungus, is a type of fungus that grows on the bodies of certain caterpillars, typically those of the moth species Orgyia leucostigma. The fungus has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits of Phalangium glaucum include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and treat various infections. In herbal preparations, the fungus is often dried and ground into a powder, which can be mixed with water or other ingredients to create a tea or tincture.
